project_name: Thomson Reserve (unofficial/marketing name) â€” redevelopment of the former Thomson View Condominium
confidence_note: HIGH confidence on the SITE/TRANSACTION: this is the redevelopment of the former Thomson View Condominium (Bright Hill Drive, District 20, Upper Thomson, beside MacRitchie Reservoir), bought via collective sale for S$810M by a UOL / Singapore Land (SingLand) / CapitaLand Development consortium (vehicle: Tamarind Development Pte Ltd); High Court sale order 1 Jul 2025, completion 2 Oct 2025. It is NOT 'The Reserve Residences' (Beauty World). LOW confidence / DISPUTED on five load-bearing details the raw research over-stated as fact: (1) the official launch NAME 'Thomson Reserve' appears only on agent/SEO microsites â€” no CapitaLand/UOL release, EdgeProp, Business Times or Straits Times confirms it (CapitaLand says plans 'announced in due course'); treat as a working/marketing name. (2) Unit count: ~1,240 (deal-stage reporting) vs 1,268 (marketing) â€” use ~1,240. (3) TOP: 2030 vs 2031 conflict, none official. (4) The indicative per-unit PRICE LIST / PSF table is marketing placeholder data the source itself labels 'indicative', mislabelled '[sourced]' in raw research â€” downgrade to [estimate]. (5) The 'Chuan Park $2,836 psf' benchmark used to argue +12% upside is wrong â€” actual launch avg was ~$2,579 psf â€” so the upside math is unreliable. Project is PRE-LAUNCH (no real sales data); showflat preview targeted ~Oct 2026.
summary: Thomson Reserve is the working name for the 99-year leasehold redevelopment of the en-bloc Thomson View Condominium site (Bright Hill Drive, D20, ~5 ha / ~504,314-540,314 sq ft, plot ratio 2.1), bought for S$810M (~S$1,178 psf ppr, inclusive of land betterment + lease upgrading premium) by UOL/SingLand/CapitaLand. Completion of the en-bloc was 2 Oct 2025; the new project (~1,240 units, 1-5 bedrooms) is pre-launch with a showflat preview targeted around Oct 2026 and no confirmed TOP (2030-2031). The investment case is a capital-appreciation play, not a yield play: first sizeable D20 new launch since JadeScape (2018), ~7-min walk to Upper Thomson MRT (TEL) with a future Bright Hill CRL interchange (~2030), Ai Tong School within 1km, and MacRitchie adjacency. Same developers executed Parktown Residence (87% sold, $2,360 psf, Feb 2025), evidencing strong launch pull. BUT: the high land rate compresses the margin of error, the official name/unit count/TOP/pricing are all still unconfirmed, a Lentor/D26 supply wave plus a 1,200-unit Upper Thomson BTO loom 2026-2027, and 99-yr leasehold caps long-run upside. Stance: SELECTIVE â€” credible site and developers, but buy on confirmed fundamentals at launch pricing, not on the marketing price table.

identity.address: Bright Hill Drive (former 1-17E), Upper Thomson, District 20, Singapore â€” beside MacRitchie Reservoir
identity.district: D20 (Upper Thomson; site sits on the Bishan/Upper Thomson fringe â€” EdgeProp classifies the former condo under 'Bishan')
identity.developer: UOL Group + Singapore Land (SingLand) + CapitaLand Development; JV vehicle 'Tamarind Development Pte Ltd' â€” structure: UOL-SingLand 80:20 vehicle 50:50 with CapitaLand Development [confirmed]
identity.site_area: ~5 ha; ~504,314 sq ft (some sources 540,314 sq ft); ~50,197 sqm [confirmed]
identity.total_units: ~1,240 (deal-stage reporting) â€” marketing sites say 1,268; treat as ~1,240-1,268, unconfirmed [disputed/estimate]
identity.site_origin: Collective sale (en-bloc) of former Thomson View Condominium â€” 255 residential units + 1 commercial, completed 1987, 99-yr lease from 7 Apr 1975 [confirmed]; (raw research's 206/254 unit figures are errors)
identity.launch_status: Pre-launch as of Jun 2026; showflat preview targeted ~Q3-Q4 2026 (Oct 2026); official pricing to follow â€” no actual sales data exists yet [confirmed]

pricing_positioning: No official pricing â€” project is unlaunched. Land rate ~S$1,178 psf ppr [confirmed] implies a breakeven in the low-S$2,000s psf (~S$2,058 psf is plausible but agent-sourced, not analyst-attributed) [estimate]. Marketing 'indicative' table spans ~$2,159-$2,454 psf across 1-5BR â€” these are placeholder figures the source labels indicative, NOT sourced launch prices [estimate/unverified]. A realistic launch band of ~S$2,300-$3,100 psf is directionally reasonable benchmarked to recent comps but unattributed [estimate]. Positioning: mid-to-upper band vs the Lentor/D26 cluster (which launched ~$1,882-$2,478 psf) and roughly parity-to-premium vs JadeScape resale (~$2,327 psf avg 2026). The 'Chuan Park $2,836 psf' anchor used in raw research is WRONG (actual launch avg ~$2,579 psf), so any '+12% upside' built on it should be discarded.

investment_thesis: First sizeable D20 new launch since JadeScape (2018) â€” ~7-8 year supply void fills pent-up upgrader demand in an established, family-oriented estate [sourced]
Strong transport: ~7-min walk to Upper Thomson MRT (TEL), near Bright Hill (future CRL interchange ~2030) and Marymount â€” multi-line, interchange-free access to Orchard/Marina Bay [confirmed]
School + nature pull: Ai Tong School within 1km plus MacRitchie Reservoir adjacency are durable, hard-to-replicate family value drivers [confirmed]
Developer execution proven: same UOL/SingLand/CapitaLand consortium sold Parktown Residence 87% at launch ($2,360 psf, Feb 2025) [confirmed]
Supply timing edge: dominates 2026 RCR new-launch supply; ~18-month first-mover window before Lentor Central/Gardens (2027) enter the same corridor [estimate]
Mega-scale en-bloc parcel (~5 ha) in land-scarce SG supports comprehensive masterplan and amenity depth [confirmed]
risks: Name, unit count (~1,240 vs 1,268), TOP (2030 vs 2031) and ALL pricing are unconfirmed â€” the marketing price table is placeholder data, not real launch pricing [disputed] â€” so any precise return math is speculative
High land rate (~$1,178 psf ppr) compresses margin of error: if launch lands at $2,600+ psf vs a low-$2,000s breakeven, early buyers have a thin cushion if resale softens to ~$2,300 psf [estimate]
Supply wave: Lentor/D26 cluster (~4,000+ units 2026-2027) plus a 1,200-unit Upper Thomson BTO (Jun 2026) raise competition and could compress rents/resale velocity [sourced]
99-yr leasehold (NOT freehold) â€” value/financing friction accrues as lease nears ~80 yrs; not a perpetual-hold asset [confirmed]
Yield, not a yield play: gross ~2.6-3.2%, net ~2.0-2.5% â€” investors must rely on multi-year capital appreciation, not rental income [estimate]
Large unit count means phased absorption â€” later phases and 1-2BR tiers may move slower at TOP given concentrated same-project resale supply [estimate]
Precedent caution: earlier Upper Thomson projects (Thomson Three/Grand) delivered only modest profits despite location, partly on layout inefficiencies [sourced]
SSD applies for properties bought from 4 Jul 2025: 16%/12%/8%/4% for sale within yr 1/2/3/4 â€” penalises any flip exit [confirmed]
payment_regulatory: Stamp duty (SG citizen, 1st property, ~S$2.5M unit): BSD ~S$94,600 (effective ~3.78%); ABSD 0%. At ~S$3.0M: BSD ~S$119,600; at ~S$3.5M: BSD ~S$149,600 [sourced: IRAS schedule]
ABSD by status: SG citizen 1st home 0%; PR 5% (1st)/30% (2nd); foreigner 60% flat (US/CH/NO/IS/LI nationals treated as citizens under FTAs) [confirmed]
Financing: LTV 75% (1st mortgage) -> ~25% down (~S$625k on S$2.5M); TDSR cap 55%; MAS stress-test rate 4% (actual fixed rates ~1.3-1.5% mid-2026) [confirmed]
Indicative servicing (S$1.875M loan): ~S$6,250/mo at 4% stress (needs gross income ~S$11.4k/mo to clear 55% TDSR); ~S$2,188/mo at ~1.4% actual [estimate]
BUC progressive payment (typical UOL/CL): 5% booking, to 20% on S&P, then stage payments (foundation/RC/walls) to 100% at TOP â€” mortgage outflow back-loaded toward completion [sourced]
SSD (bought from 4 Jul 2025): 16%/12%/8%/4%/0% for disposal within 1/2/3/4/4+ years [confirmed]
location: Bright Hill Drive, D20, Upper Thomson â€” beside MacRitchie Reservoir (Central Catchment Nature Reserve) [confirmed]
MRT: ~7-min walk to Upper Thomson (TE8, TEL); near Bright Hill (TE7, future CRL interchange ~2030) and Marymount (CC16) [confirmed/estimate on CRL timing]
Expressways: CTE / SLE / PIE network accessible; ~10-min drive to Bishan corridor [estimate]
Schools: Ai Tong School within 1km; also CHIJ St Nicholas Girls, Catholic High, Peirce/Whitley/Mayflower Sec, Eunoia JC, Raffles Institution ~2km [confirmed on Ai Tong; others sourced]
Retail/amenity: Thomson Plaza (~500m), Junction 8 & AMK Hub (~10-15 min), Mount Alvernia Hospital nearby [sourced]
Nature/recreation: direct MacRitchie trails/TreeTop Walk; Lower Peirce, Bishan-AMK Park, Rail Corridor within short drive [sourced]
demand_supply: No take-up data â€” project unlaunched. March 2026 comparable launches were very strong (Pinery 92.5%, River Modern 90%), implying healthy absorption potential [sourced]
Thomson Reserve is the only mega-scale RCR launch in 2026 â€” supply scarcity favours strong early take-up [estimate]; large unit count means later-phase absorption may slow
Direct future competition: Lentor Central (~562 units, 2027) and Lentor Gardens (~500-530 units, late 2026/2027) enter the same TEL corridor â€” first-mover advantage matters [sourced/estimate]
Public-housing overhang: 1,200-unit Upper Thomson BTO (Jun 2026, first in 40+ yrs) + AMK BTO pull some upgrader demand, but staggered 2028-2030 delivery softens near-term impact [sourced]

Rental: est gross yield ~3.0-3.8% (1-2BR strongest), net ~2.0-2.5%; tenant base = expats (tech/life-sciences/CBD via TEL) + Ai Tong-catchment families; HDB MOP supply surge is a mild headwind [estimate]
Oversupply risk LOW-MODERATE for RCR 2026-2027; competition risk MODERATE from Lentor cluster [estimate]

verification.claim: Site is the former Thomson View en-bloc (Bright Hill Drive, D20); NOT The Reserve Residences/Beauty World
verification.note: Wee Swee Teow (handling firm), CapitaLand release, EdgeProp, 99.co, ETC all corroborate the site and transaction

verification.claim: Official project name is 'Thomson Reserve' (renamed from Thomson View Residences Jan 2026)
verification.note: Name appears ONLY on agent/SEO microsites and PR wires; no developer release or reputable media confirms it. CapitaLand says plans 'announced in due course'. Treat as unofficial working name

verification.claim: Developer consortium = UOL + SingLand + CapitaLand Development (vehicle Tamarind Development Pte Ltd)
verification.note: Wee Swee Teow names the consortium; structure UOL-SingLand 80:20 then 50:50 with CapitaLand Development (CapitaLand's solo release names CLD+UOL 50:50, SingLand inside UOL's half)

verification.claim: En-bloc price S$810M (~S$1,178 psf ppr); High Court order 1 Jul 2025; completion 2 Oct 2025; reserve was S$918M
verification.note: Wee Swee Teow + ETC + 99.co + EdgeProp; ~12% below original reserve; largest court-sanctioned collective sale since Chuan Park's S$890M (2023)

verification.claim: Fresh 99-year leasehold; former condo had 255 residential + 1 commercial unit (1987, 99-yr from 1975)
verification.note: ETC/EdgeProp/99.co. Raw research's 206 and 254 unit figures are errors; correct is 255+1. New project is NOT freehold
Better-supported figure is ~1,240 (deal-stage reporting); 1,268 appears mainly on marketing sites. No developer-confirmed count yet
Sources conflict 2030 vs 2031; no developer-confirmed date. Should be an estimate, not sourced

verification.claim: Indicative unit-mix price list / PSF table (e.g. 1BR $2,454 psf; range $2,159-$2,454) marked [sourced: launch pricing]
verification.note: Source itself states pricing is indicative and official prices come near the Oct 2026 preview. Placeholder/marketing estimates with false precision â€” downgrade to [estimate]

verification.claim: Chuan Park benchmark ~$2,836 psf, used to argue +12% upside
verification.note: Actual Chuan Park launch avg ~$2,579 psf (76% of 916 units, Nov 2024, EdgeProp). The $2,836 figure is unsupported; derived upside math is unreliable
EdgeProp â€” 400/533 units over 15-16 Mar 2025. Raw research mis-dated this as Mar 2024

verification.claim: Parktown Residence (same developers) 87% sold at $2,360 psf, Feb 2025
verification.note: CapitaLand/UOL releases + EdgeProp; 1,041/1,193 units â€” strongest direct developer comp

verification.claim: Pre-launch; showflat preview ~Oct 2026; no actual sales/take-up data
verification.note: Consistent across all sources incl. independent; logically there can be no sales data yet

verdict.detail: The site, transaction (S$810M, ~$1,178 psf ppr) and developer pedigree (UOL/SingLand/CapitaLand, Parktown 87% sold) are genuinely strong, and the location (TEL, Ai Tong, MacRitchie, first big D20 launch since 2018) supports a capital-appreciation thesis. But the official NAME, unit count (~1,240 vs 1,268), TOP (2030/2031) and ALL pricing remain UNCONFIRMED â€” the circulating price table is marketing placeholder, not real launch pricing â€” and the high land rate leaves a thin error margin against a looming Lentor + Upper-Thomson-BTO supply wave on a 99-yr lease. Verdict: a credible upgrader/owner-occupier capital-growth buy IF launch pricing prints near comparables (~mid-$2,000s psf) with a 7+ year hold; AVOID for yield-seekers and flippers (gross yield ~3%, SSD drag). Decide at the Oct 2026 preview on confirmed specs and official price, not on the marketing figures.

data_gaps: Official project name not confirmed by any developer/reputable-media source
Final unit count (~1,240 vs 1,268) and unit mix unconfirmed
No official pricing, floor plans, or showflat location â€” all current price/PSF figures are marketing estimates
New lease commencement date (will run from completion, not 1975) not disclosed
Bright Hill CRL interchange exact opening (~2030) and CRL Phase 3 alignment near Central Catchment still subject to LTA confirmation
